expectations.text: Permission string in route _permission EXACTLY matches the string defined in .permissions.yml (orphaned/mismatched permission strings silently deny access to everyone -- no error, no warning, just a 403 for all users)
expectations.evidence: restricted_reports.permissions.yml line 1 defines 'view restricted reports'. restricted_reports.routing.yml line 7 uses _permission: 'view restricted reports'. These strings match exactly.

expectations.text: If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects with ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or uses AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() which adds contexts automatically (bare AccessResult without cache contexts gets cached for the first user's result and applies to ALL subsequent users)
expectations.evidence: No custom access checking code exists. grep for 'AccessResult' in the module returned no matches. The module uses only route-level _permission requirement, which handles caching correctly by default. The 'If' condition is not triggered.

expectations.text: If AccessResult::allowedIf() or AccessResult::forbidden() is used directly, it includes ->addCacheContexts() call (NOT bare AccessResult without cache metadata -- this is a security and caching bug where access decisions bleed across users)
expectations.evidence: No AccessResult::allowedIf() or AccessResult::forbidden() usage found anywhere in the module. grep for 'AccessResult' returned no matches. The 'If' condition is not triggered.

expectations.text: Does NOT use hook_permission() to define permissions (D7 pattern removed in D8 -- permissions must be in .permissions.yml)
expectations.evidence: grep for 'hook_permission' in the module returned no matches. No .module file exists. Permissions are correctly defined in restricted_reports.permissions.yml.

expectations.text: Does NOT use _access: 'TRUE' on a route that should be permission-restricted (opens route to everyone including anonymous)
expectations.evidence: grep for "_access:\s*'TRUE'" in the module returned no matches. restricted_reports.routing.yml uses _permission: 'view restricted reports' which properly restricts access.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: ddev drush en restricted_reports -y returned 'Already installed: restricted_reports' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: Navigate to /reports/restricted WITHOUT logging in -- verify an Access Denied / 403 response (proves anonymous access is properly blocked) (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: Browser report: HTTP Status 403, Page title 'Access denied | Drush Site-Install'. Anonymous access is properly blocked.

expectations.text: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /reports/restricted -- verify the page renders with content and HTTP 200 (proves permission-based access grants correctly) (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: Browser report: HTTP Status 200, Page title 'Restricted Reports | Drush Site-Install', Content: heading and descriptive text rendered correctly.

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: ddev exec vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice --extensions=php,module,install,yml web/modules/custom/restricted_reports/ returned exit code 0 with no output (no errors).
